title: HealthCheckResponse
description: Canonical JSON body returned by any harness-managed application's health endpoint. Agents poll this endpoint using HealthCheckSpec (harness_skills.boot) to verify an isolated instance is alive and ready before sending test traffic.

status.description: Coarse health verdict. 'healthy' means all checks passed and the instance is ready to serve traffic. 'degraded' means the instance is running but one or more non-critical checks failed — agents should proceed with caution. 'unhealthy' means a critical subsystem is down — agents must not send test traffic and should trigger a re-boot or alert.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when this response was generated. Agents use this to detect stale cached responses.

version.description: Application version string (semver, git SHA, or build tag). Agents use this to confirm the expected code revision is running after a deploy.

uptime_s.description: Seconds the process has been running. Useful for detecting unexpected restarts.

checks.description: Ordered list of individual subsystem checks. At least one check should be present. Agents iterate this list to identify which specific subsystem is failing before deciding on a recovery action.

CheckStatus.description: pass = subsystem is healthy; fail = subsystem is down or unreachable; warn = subsystem is degraded but not completely unavailable.

CheckResult.description: Result of probing one subsystem (database, cache, external API, queue, etc.).

name.description: Short identifier for this check. Agents match on this field when diagnosing failures.

latency_ms.description: Round-trip latency to probe this subsystem in milliseconds. null when the check did not involve a remote call.

message.description: Human- and agent-readable detail. On failure: concrete error message. On pass: optional diagnostic note (e.g. 'pool_size=10/20'). Agents surface this when deciding whether to retry or escalate.

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code for automated remediation routing (e.g. 'DB_CONNECTION_REFUSED', 'MIGRATION_PENDING', 'DISK_FULL'). null on pass or when no code is defined.

InstanceInfo.description: Metadata about the running instance. Agents use this to correlate a health response with the correct worktree when multiple isolated instances are running concurrently.

worktree_id.description: Harness worktree ID injected at boot (mirrors BootConfig.worktree_id). Agents validate this matches the expected worktree before accepting the health response.

port.description: TCP port this instance is bound to (mirrors IsolationConfig.port).

pid.description: OS process ID. Agents can cross-check this against the PID returned in BootResult.

git_sha.description: Full or abbreviated git commit SHA of the running code. Agents use this to verify the expected revision was deployed.

git_branch.description: Git branch name at build / launch time.

db_schema.description: Active database schema or SQLite file path used by this instance (mirrors IsolationConfig.db_schema / db_file). Helps agents confirm the correct isolated DB is in use.

environment.description: Runtime environment name (e.g. 'test', 'staging', 'production'). Agents assert this is never 'production' during automated testing.
